heading: Reporting of background check denials to State authorities
label: 925B Reporting of background check denials to State authorities
notes: Effective DateSection not effective until Oct. 1 of the first fiscal year beginning after , see , set out as a note under , Commerce and Trade.Mar. 15, 2022section 4(a) of div. W of Pub. L. 117–103section 6851 of Title 15

children.label: B
children.text: the Federal, State, local or Tribal prohibition;

children.label: C
children.text: the date and time the notice was provided;

children.label: D
children.text: the location of the licensee where the firearm was sought to be transferred; and

children.label: E
children.text: the identity of the person; and
where practicable, report the incident to State and local prosecutors or Tribal prosecutors in the jurisdiction where the firearm transfer was sought.

children.heading: .—Requirements for Report
children.label: b .—Requirements for Report
children.text: A report is made in accordance with this subsection if the report is made under subsection (a) within 24 hours after the NICS denies a firearm transfer in accordance with , United States Code, except that the making of the report may be delayed for so long as is necessary to avoid compromising an ongoing investigation.section 922(t) of title 18

children.heading: .—Amendment of Report
children.label: c .—Amendment of Report
children.text: If a report is made in accordance with subsection (b) and, after such report is made, the Federal Bureau of Investigation determines that the receipt of a firearm by a person for whom the report was made would not violate subsection (g) or (n) of section 922 or State, local, or Tribal law, the Attorney General shall notify any law enforcement authority and any prosecutor to whom the report was made of that determination.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: d .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in subsection (a) shall be construed to require a report with respect to a person to be made to the same State authorities that made the original denial determination with respect to the transfer of the firearm.
